summary: The focused Stage 3 renderer repair is implemented for the artifact-create landing verdict work_item metadata gap.
implementation_summary: Added work_item to the landing_verdict renderer metadata sequence so generated landing verdict artifacts include the parser-required work item field.

Aligned the focused artifact-create landing verdict fixtures with current landing verdict parser values for operator_input_status and landing_agent_state, preserving the parser-compatibility check without changing landing policy.

Kept the repair limited to src/commands/artifact-create-renderers.ts and focused artifact-create test data; no hidden authority, landing policy, UAT policy, cockpit, scheduler, claim, worktree, PR/CI, dependency, lockfile, or supply-chain-sensitive surface was introduced.

clean_code_check.evidence: The renderer now emits work_item metadata for landing_verdict artifacts, matching the active BANDIT-035 acceptance criteria and existing landing-verdict parser contract.
The production diff adds one metadata field to the existing landing verdict field list; test changes only update fixture metadata to current accepted parser values.
artifact-create remains a renderer from explicit structured input into repo-native Markdown evidence and does not advance workflow state beyond the existing lifecycle event.
The focused artifact-create test now proves the generated landing verdict includes work_item and remains accepted by bandit validate.
Existing artifact-create refusal-path tests for unsupported kinds, occupied outputs, unknown work items, and out-of-repo specs continue to pass.

next_action: Run Stage 4 review gates for BANDIT-035: pre-PR CodeRabbit, Local Qwen, aggregate review evidence, and PM disposition at the current review subject hash.
